title: planchonella rigidifolia
intro: Planchonella rigidifolia, also known as the 'Yellow Sapote', is a species of evergreen tree native to the tropical rainforests of New Guinea. This plant has been found to have various medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Planchonella rigidifolia include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Planchonella rigidifolia can be made into a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often used in traditional medicine in Papua New Guinea.
